campus: Online Class (using video conferencing tools). Students must log into CANVAS on May 20, 2026, from 10:00 A.M. to 11:00 A.M. and click “Announcements” for Online Orientation Information. Students must attend class online via the College’s online platform CANVAS, have a laptop or desktop; and have reliable Internet access. THE CONTENT FOR THIS CLASS IS DELIVERED COMPLETELY ONLINE. STUDENTS ARE NOT REQUIRED TO ATTEND ON-CAMPUS SESSIONS. STUDENTS COMPLETE COURSE REQUIREMENTS WITHIN A WINDOW OF TIME DETERMINED BY THE INSTRUCTOR. FAILURE TO COMPLETE THE ATTENDANCE VERIFICATION ASSIGNMENT DURING THE FIRST WEEK OF CLASS MAY AFFECT ENROLLMENT IN THE COURSE AND/OR FINANCIAL AID BENEFITS.

Online Class. Virtual classes (using video conferencing tools) meet online (remotely) during regularly scheduled times weekly. Students enrolled must attend class online via the College’s online platform CANVAS, have a laptop or desktop, and have reliable Internet access. Students must log into CANVAS on the first day of class at 9:00AM and click “Announcements” for Online Orientation Information. THE CONTENT FOR THIS CLASS IS DELIVERED COMPLETELY ONLINE. STUDENTS ARE NOT REQUIRED TO ATTEND ON-CAMPUS SESSIONS. STUDENTS COMPLETE COURSE REQUIREMENTS WITHIN A WINDOW OF TIME DETERMINED BY THE INSTRUCTOR. FAILURE TO COMPLETE THE ATTENDANCE VERIFICATION ASSIGNMENT DURING THE FIRST WEEK OF CLASS MAY AFFECT ENROLLMENT IN THE COURSE AND/OR FINANCIAL AID BENEFITS.

Hybrid course. Students will receive lecture content online and on campus during the designated day(s) and time(s) listed. Students must attend class online via the College's online platform CANVAS. FAILURE TO ATTEND ONLINE AND IN PERSON CLASS MEETINGS MAY AFFECT ENROLLMENT IN THE COURSE AND/OR FINANCIAL AID BENEFITS.
